title: Filed Abstract: Scene

sections.body:
The atelier of Émile Sauval, painter, lay in that equivocal state which follows a death unattended by clergy or kin. A half-finished canvas—a woman’s face blurred as though seen through rain—stared at a ceiling stained by years of candle-reek. Upon the floor, a glass of cognac overturned, its contents long since drunk or spilled, but a fine white sediment clung to the inner curve like frost upon a winter pane. The decanter stood upon a sideboard, innocent to the eye, yet the laboratory would later pronounce it clean. Sauval himself had fallen from his stool, easel toppling, a smear of lead-white upon his cuff where he had ground his own pigments. The hour of death, whispered the medical examiner, fell somewhere between the nineteenth and twentieth hour—a wide gate, through which many alibis might pass.

The door-book, that punctilious register of arrivals and departures, recorded five visitors between ten in the morning and seven of the evening. Each name was signed, each time noted, each exit attested. Yet the desk-note beneath it read only: "keep apart from the louder papers." A prudent reader will wonder why a document so ordinary should be sequestered from the common file.

Entered as sober truth, though truth oft weareth a counterfeit coat.

subtitle: Entered as sober truth, though truth oft weareth a counterfeit coat.
